content.problemStatement:
**Q2) OR B):** You are working on an inventory management system. The system provides a list of items recorded in a single line. This list is a mix of item quantities (integers) and item descriptions (strings). Your task is to write a Python program that processes this list and calculates the total sum of all item quantities.

You must implement a function that iterates through the given list. This function should add up all the integer values while gracefully ignoring the string descriptions. You are required to use a `try-except` block to handle the type mismatch when an element is not a valid integer.


Input Format
A single line of text containing space-separated values. These values can be integers or strings.



Output Format
A single integer representing the sum of all the integer values found in the input list.
